properties.remark: A SCREEN DOOR WAS TORN FROM A HOME ALONG HWY 92E IN MONTICELLO. A NUMBER OF POWER OUTAGES WERE ALSO REPORTED IN AND AROUND MONTICELLO. MULTIPLE TREES HAD BEEN BLOWN DOWN ON HWYS 90E AND 92E IN MONTICELLO.
A TREE FELL ON A CARPORT DAMAGING BOTH CARPORT AND BOAT BEING HOUSED INSIDE THE CARPORT. ANOTHER TREE ALSO FELL ON A BUILDING AT ANOTHER SITE IN KEAVY AND DAMAGED THAT BUILDING AS WELL.
SOME SMALL TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN ALONG HWY 92E NEAR MONTICELLO.
ESTIMATED 58 MPH WIND GUST IN NED.
ONE TREE DOWN ON BROWN CREEK ROAD NEAR 451 AND HAL ROGERS PARKWAY AT MILE MARKER 56.

THE ESTILL COUNTY HIGHWAY DEPT REPORTED THAT NUMEROUS TREES AND POWER LINES HAD BEEN BLOWN DOWN ACROSS THE COUNTY. AREAS AFFECTED INCLUDED RAVENNA, WAGERSVILLE, MCKEE, COW CREEK, AND RICHMOND RD. POWER OUTAGES WERE ALSO REPORTED ACROSS THE COUNTY.
POWER OUTAGES WERE REPORTED IN CALABOOSE.
ESTIL COUNTY EM RELAYED REPORT OF NUMEROUS TREES BLOWN DOWN AROUND THE COUNTY BETWEEN ABOUT 7 AND 10 A.M. 4 TO 5 THOUSAND PEOPLE WERE WITHOUT POWER DURING THE MORNING HOURS.
TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN ON WINCHESTER ROAD JUST NORTH OF THE TOWN OF PALMER ON THE ESTILL CLARK COUNTY LINE.
POWELL COUNTY EM RELAYED NUMEROUS REPORTS OF LARGE TREES DOWN AND SEVERAL OUT BUILDINGS DAMAGED OR HAD ROOFS BLOWN OFF AROUND THE HARDWICK CREEK AREA.
POWER OUTAGES AROUND THE COUNTY. NUMEROUS REPORTS OF TREES DOWN THROUGHOUT THE COUNTY.

TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN ON HWY 1010 IN TOLIVER.

LARGE TREES DOWN ON KY 1010 AND ALONG THE MOUNTAIN PARKWAY FROM THUNDERSTORM WINDS.
POWER OUTAGES WERE REPORTED IN CALABOOSE.
OVERHANG PORCH AND PIECES OF SIDING TORN OFF A MOBILE HOME.

EF-1 TORNADO. TORNADO PATH ONE QUARTER OF A MILE LONG AND 100 YARDS WIDE. NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED AND SCATTERED. ONE MOBILE HOME HAD ITS ROOF BLOW OFF AND WAS PUSHED ABOUT 5 FEET OFF ITS FOUNDATION. ONE BARN WAS COMPLETELY DESTROYED. TWO MOBILE HOMES HAD ROOF AND SIDING TORN OFF. ONE OUTBUILDING USED AS A SAWMILL WAS DESTROYED.
A SPOTTER REPORTED THAT HIS LAWN FURNITURE WAS BEING BLOWN AROUND BY THUNDERSTORM WIND GUSTS 3 MILES SOUTH OF CANEL CITY. PEA SIZE HAIL WAS ALSO FALLING WHEN THE REPORT WAS CALLED IN.
TREE BRANCHES THE SIZE OF A PERSONS ARM WERE BEING BLOWN DOWN IN THE COMMUNITY OF PULASKI SOUTH OF EUBANK.
THE LEE COUNTY SUPERINTENDENT REPORTED THAT ALL OF THE SHOOLS COUNTY WIDE WERE WITHOUT POWER DUE TO THUNDERSTORM WIND GUSTS.

THE PULASKI COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGER RECEIVED NUMEROUS REPORTS OF DOWNED TREES AND STRUCTURAL DAMAGE FROM AROUND THE COUNTY. REPORTS WERE RECEIVED OF SHINGLES BEING BLOWN OFF ROOFS AND A CAR PORT WAS BLOWN INTO A STREET CAUSING DAMAGE TO AN OCCUPIED CAR.
SEVERAL LARGE TREES DOWN OR SNAPPED ALONG THE MOUNTAIN PARKWAY NEAR MILE MARKER 68.

ONE DOUBLE WIDE TRAILER WAS BLOWN OVER AND HAD ROOF BLOWN OFF. ONE SINGLE WIDE TRAILER HAD MOST OF ITS ROOF BLOWN OFF. ONE LARGE APPLE TREE WAS UPROOTED. THE DAMAGE HAPPENED AT LONG BRACH JUST WEST OF SALYERSVILLE.
ONE TREE WAS BLOWN DOWN ON KY HIGHWAY 30. THE TIME IS BASED ON RADAR ESTIMATE.
ONE TRAILER BLOW OVER WITHIN THE CITY LIMITS OF SALYERSVILLE.
